message:
fix(planner,vdbe): upgrade INTEGER PRIMARY KEY point lookups to SeekRowid

The planner's `classify_where_term` only recognised `rowid`, `_rowid_`,
and `oid` as rowid aliases, so `WHERE id = ?` on a table declared with
`id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY` was classified as a plain Equality term rather
than RowidEquality.  As a result, `best_access_path` returned
FullTableScan and the VDBE emitted a linear Rewind+Next scan instead of
a O(log N) SeekRowid.

Fix: in `planner_select_directive`, post-process the result of
`best_access_path` — when it returns FullTableScan, check whether any
WHERE Equality term refers to the table's IPK column (identified via
`col.is_ipk` from the schema).  If so, upgrade the access path to
RowidLookup before passing it to the VDBE codegen, which then emits
SeekRowid.

This is safe because an INTEGER PRIMARY KEY column is always a unique
rowid alias in SQLite's storage model.

Benchmark impact (operation_baseline_bench on 1000-row table):
  op_point_lookup: 116,410 ns → 5,032 ns  (23x improvement, 269x → 12x vs C SQLite)
  All other operations: within measurement noise.

Fixes #43 (partial — remaining gap from re-parsing and bytecode
re-compilation overhead, not B-tree seek inefficiency).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
